At a fireside chat at HBS, Aravind Srinivas, CEO of Perplexity AI, emphasized a truth-seeking, fast-moving culture as the company scales. He sees AI as a reasoning partner, not just an answer tool, and positioned Perplexity as a cleaner, more direct alternative to Google’s ad-heavy search. He dismissed Apple acquisition rumors, called for better fact-checking on TikTok, and praised open source efforts like DeepSeek for ensuring LLM accountability. You can find more of Aravind's insights in this post.[Digital Data Design (D^3) Institute at Harvard](https://www.linkedin.com/company/d3instituteatharvard?trk=public_post-text)[Harvard Business School](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-business-school/?trk=public_post-text)[Harvard University](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-university/?trk=public_post-text)[Harvard Innovation Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-innovation-labs/?trk=public_post-text)[MTLC](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/mtlc-conseil?trk=public_post-text)[Patrick S. While I'm still coming closer to the UX itself, before it we have to understand the way how we proceed with the information. I talked about perception, now I want to talk about memory, which is important to keep in mind while you are thinking about an experience overall.
- Did you know that we forget about 40% of new information after only 20 minutes, and 70% just the next day?
- Or did you know that our brain can alter our memory? And we can remember things which never actually happened!
- Did you know that our working memory can hold about 4–5 items for adults and 2-3 items for children according to recent research?
- To remember something and transfer it to long-term memory, we have to record it in detail, through meaningful use or association.
- Also, emotion can make experiences and information more memorable.
